post.title: Execution Week
post.body:
Ops OS is done. Full team adoption — a genuine mega success. Thank you for leaning in.

Now we execute. I'm back from OOO and already in motion. CHIPS officially kicks off this week — first real work sessions. Canva GTM outbound starts, with Anja and Jen leading the charge on AE outreach. Demand gen stack ranking lands today and drives the next two weeks of GTM quick wins.

Today's all-hands huddle includes the first major milestone on the team's AI skills journey: company-wide AI demo day is officially announced. The next three weeks are practical and business-focused. The goal is AI that solves real problems — not experiments, but outcomes.

week.text: Ops OS rollout complete — full team adoption achieved, mega success
CHIPS kicks off — first real work sessions begin this week
Canva GTM outbound launch — Anja and Jen leading AE cold outreach program
Demand gen stack ranking — Niket finalizing prioritized list; drives next two weeks of GTM execution
Company-wide AI demo day announced — first showcase of team AI work

month.text: Demand gen / outbound system online and consistent
Canva GTM live — begin outbounding Canva AEs
Consolidated Comms code complete; in testing (target: end of May)
CHIPS alpha build kicks off — Week 21
AI COS Night Class transitioning to on-demand course for all FTEs

reading.title: 40 MCP Servers That Turn Claude Into a Productivity Machine
Introducing Canva AI 2.0: Reimagining how the world creates
Ethereum Whitepaper — Smart Contracts & Decentralized Apps
The 9 Levels of Leadership Every Founder Should Understand
The Most Boring Billion-Dollar Businesses of 2027

signals.text: Net MRR: -$20,002 (week of 5/11), down sharply from +$8,327 the prior week. New MRR: +$18,363; Churn: -$14,536 (improved); Contraction: -$27,546 (widened); Expansion: +$3,717 (collapsed from +$23,414). Two consecutive weeks of historic demo lows: 19 created / 17 completed vs. 60+ target. Working with Bryan on recovery; OA restart is the primary lever.

Ambral concerns: 300+ signals is too many — majority are duplicative of what the team already knows. Intercom integration not working as intended. $45k annual commitment was signed without being communicated internally. When signals become proactive, will need better integration with existing tools.

10 client-facing calls this week: 6 onboardings (On Air Brands, LaundroBOOST, SpotHero, Orangellow, Bluespring Wealth, Zeus Fire & Security) + 4 account syncs. AI-assisted churn review running across portfolio using Ambral.

Critical churn risk — Commonwealth Health: active cancellation risk. Client flagged brand mixing (Commonwealth Health assets delivered with Tenor Health branding), quality regression since designated designer → team transition, and is 'looking at other options.' Two new Jira tickets opened 5/15. Downgrade to monthly plan requested on renewal. Needs immediate personal outreach and clear action plan.
Cancellation confirmed — Yotta Event: cancel set for July 8th. Upgraded in April, new user onboarding escalated in May, now canceling. July 8 window remains for a save conversation if warranted.

Escalating quality risk — Immersive Gamebox: two quality issues in two weeks from a client who upgraded a month ago. Latest LFR: 'looked terrible.' Escalated to Sara Giraldo for AD support review — in progress.

Multi-year escalation — Orangellow: two calls this week (5/11 + 5/14 with full team). Client left a review and requested the meeting. Ongoing issues: cost, quality, speed. Open action items from SD-17441 (AD review + check-in call) still on hold — these are critical next actions.

Exploring Intercom AI tools to streamline CS workflows. Building Intercom reports for leads and Picklebot responses. Getting into Ambral to catch up on churn signals and prepare for broader rep onboarding.

Client onboarding and check-in calls, Ambral churn risk identification underway. Win: de-escalated a new customer whose account was not activated in time for their onboarding call. Win: successful onboarding with follow-up calls scheduled for a short-term urgent account.
Consolidated comms is code complete. Now in testing and bug-fix phase ahead of internal testing. On track for end-of-May target.
CHIPS officially kicks off this week. First real work sessions begin — Jar roadmap lock freed up bandwidth to re-engage. This is now the primary lane alongside GTM support.

Collected overdue $30k invoice from Canva — resolved. Meeting with James today (rescheduled from last week). Cold outreach to Canva AEs planned for after Memorial Day. Note: Canva requesting DP upload client list into their system for AE upsell outreach — not recommended; one-sided and creates unsolicited outreach risk. Needs further discussion.
Meeting with Jen today to launch AE cold outreach program. Claude integrations for sales live and in active use. Hit 50% MRR weekly target via pipeline build-out last week.

Following up with Meg Moss on partnership proposal. Affinity transition: Canva wants DP to define 'what good looks like' (training, press release, revenue targets) — response pending from Meg. ENT Studio conversations expected to resume to confirm 2026 vs. 2027 scope.

HubSpot renewal in negotiation — two-part cost reduction strategy: (1) Rev Hero for CS meeting calendars (cheaper alternative to HubSpot seats for non-sales staff), (2) reducing marketing contacts with Empty Cup. Both in progress with Alesha.
Gong AI rollout paused — engineering prioritizing AE/Salesforce integration first; CSMs are next after AEs complete.

Company-wide AI demo day announced at today's all-hands huddle — first formal milestone in the team's AI skills journey. Teams will present where they are and inspire each other. Goal: practical, business-focused AI execution over the next three weeks.
MBR tomorrow (May 19) — reviewing May performance and setting the next two weeks' priorities. Niket presenting AI-powered data analysis as a personal challenge.
Demand gen stack ranking: Niket finalizing prioritized list today. IDS item for leadership meeting. Output drives next two weeks of GTM quick wins from the brainstorm session.

Attended AI COS Session 4 (Full Autonomy, May 17). Takeaway: clarity on when to use Claude vs. AI Chief of Staff for contextual tasks, plus maintenance protocols (weekly updates, automated Sunday systems review). Applying to sales AI tooling scale.
AI COS Night Class transitioning to an on-demand course accessible to all FTEs next week. Ops OS rollout: 100% team adoption — complete.

Net MRR: +$8,327 WoW (improved from -$12,927), but MTD barely +$333. Lead volume at historic lows — closed-won deals are coming from older pipeline, not new leads. Pipeline impact will hurt through rest of May; AOP target at risk if outbound and inbound don't recover.
Demos: 30 created / 17 completed vs. 60+ target. Churn improved to -$18,887 (from -$31,551) but 95% retention not met for second consecutive week. Contraction MRR widened to -$25,105.
Ambral refinements compiled from CS team — 9 key adjustments to flagging logic (support ticket signals, cancel policy, CSM assignment, Slack silence, etc.). Official rollout still pending.
Pomelli pilot active for 2–3 hour accounts — OB team generating internal brand profiles before first request is worked on. Onboarding flow update proposal submitted.
Ending MRR $1,678,080. NRR 97.8%. April close underway. May COGS target discussion with Rob ongoing — likely to come in above given month is underway.

JAR AI 1+3-month roadmap LOCKED and shipped. Phase 1 = Brand strategy + Brand identity only (Brand DNA: identity, strategy, tone of voice, photography). JAR AI pricing = free minus LLM costs for horizontal expansion. BYOT promoted to a service rail.
Consolidated Comms at 64% this week — ahead of end-of-May target. 2/3hr customer decision resolved (single shared CS user pattern). Bug fixes in existing Jar experience complete.
3 external engineering resources active (Neutech, Irya, Svemir). Take-home assessments sent to Neutech + Irya. Neutech shortlist: 2 Snr + 1 Jnr. Irya NDA signed.
Open Jar UI/UX phase 1 flow near completion. Canva GTM landing page and ads in progress. Social calendar approved and in flight.

Brief Butler initiative kicked off — creative brief quality identified as the highest-impact client problem. Working session Monday with Rob, Janelle, Lacey. Janelle: 'It's the biggest problem actually.'
Deel integration PRFAQ complete — team aligned on check-in/checkout system for designer availability and blackout logic. Ledger first ticket in progress (PI-13).
CHIPS delayed to Week 21 — this week is OOO prep and GTM handoffs. Jar roadmap lock frees up the bandwidth to re-engage next week.
Canva GTM campaign (website, ads, consultative concept) in final handoff to sales this week. Goal: begin outbounding Canva AEs. Retargeting micro-targeting ads also in scope.

Partner agreement sent by Canva to Niket — tracking it down. Adobe → Affinity transition: Canva wants DP to define 'what good looks like' (training, press release, revenue targets). Action: Russ, Rob, Jen this week.

Lost K. Hovnanian deal on pricing — Canva referral quoted at $25K for 200 pages. Recommendation: re-examine pricing for zero-CAC Canva direct referrals. ENT Studio work from Canva pushed to ~2027 (215 clients, 15 pages each).
Pipeline build toward 50% MRR target. Tighter marketing/sales feedback loop in progress. Claude integrations for sales workflow underway.
Zoom renewal resolved at significant cost reduction. HubSpot negotiation in progress — vendor not acknowledging cost constraints; timeline allows continued negotiation.
April close in progress. Commission deliverables for RevOps + JaCoby. Snowflake/Tableau refresh pipeline being triaged and documented — no single owner remains.
Demand gen / outbound declared company-wide priority. Focus is the #1 lever. All GTM-adjacent leaders rallying around Bryan + team with data, insights, and execution.
Ops OS at ~95% team adoption — one more week to full rollout. AI COS Night Class next cohort launching end of May (free for FTEs — reach out if interested).
Ops OS Windows install proven end-to-end. Anja, Hollie, Anya all onboarded via simplified Claude-driven SSH key flow. No GitHub Desktop required.
Net MRR: -$43,943. Churn ($112K) outpacing new MRR ($71K) — churn reduction is the #1 lever this month.
April demos: 80% completion rate (129/162). Ambral fully active on all accounts; HubSpot duplication friction being resolved.
Auto Assigner fully shipped — single queue, give-back auto-run, revision logic. Canva % of tickets: 24.4% and rising.
Adobe contract negotiation live (meeting May 1). Customer sensitivity analysis near complete.
JAR AI go/no-go = go. 1/2/3 month roadmap in build mode. Architecture: branding module as a separate, public-facing surface — AI-driven creative briefing interface as first consumer surface.
3 external engineering resources now active: Neutech (Snr shortlist set), Irya Solutions (NDA signed, first initiative prepping), Svemir (commercials pending).
Consolidated Comms: first live demo delivered, scope right-sized (~75% cut). Day-one = single Client:DP channel. Delivery in progress.
Jar formally handed to Arnoe this week. Roadmap review 90% complete with Arnoe + Janco.
First full week back on CHIPS. More material update after this week's work sessions.
QBR in Austin: packaging & pricing landed well with Meg Moss. Direct access to Canva's sales team now open.
Updated Canva sales plan drafted — reviewing with leaders this week and handing off. Partnership plan with Meg formalizing.
Canva transition communicated to creative leads. Canva volume staffing model in progress with Janelle.
Investigating creative contractor COGS creep. ETL/Tableau audit underway — no single owner remains, documenting institutional knowledge.
Intercom renewal signed at significant discount + seat reduction. HubSpot negotiation ongoing.
Chapter Three strategy locked. Lanes & objectives revision + team assignments with Niket this week — stalled last week due to board cycles.
Ops OS rolling company-wide this week. Windows install path not yet end-to-end — running old machine as test rig for Niket's install.
